parameter.q: the SOQL statement, URL-encoded; spaces may travel as + or %20 — the decoded value must equal the SOQL exactly

response.totalSize: number — full result-set count, constant across pages
response.done: boolean — true only on the final page
response.nextRecordsUrl: opaque server-issued path, present only while done is false, e.g. /services/data/v67.0/query/01gKB0000016PIAYA2-2000
response.records: array of record objects for this batch (default batch up to 2,000 records)

record_shape.fields: the selected fields as siblings of attributes (Id, Name, ...)

cursor_rules: request nextRecordsUrl exactly as issued against the instance origin — never rebuild it, never re-append q or any other parameter
keep requesting until a response reports done true; a page without nextRecordsUrl and done false is a contract breach
records keep their attributes type/url metadata in the export, in server order, each exactly once

error_envelope.client_rule: non-2xx responses other than the handled 401/503 cases raise SalesforceApiException with StatusCode/ErrorCode/Message; the access token never appears in exception text

session_refresh.policy: call the session source's RefreshTokenAsync at most ONCE per export run, then repeat the identical URL with the new token; a 401 after the refresh raises SalesforceSessionException
session_refresh.note: local policy pinned by this seed

throttle_retry.policy: wait exactly Retry-After seconds via the injected delay (never Task.Delay when one is provided), then repeat the identical URL at most once per page; a second consecutive 503 on the same page raises SalesforceApiException
throttle_retry.no_duplicates: a page that fails and then succeeds on retry contributes its records exactly once — the export must never contain a duplicate record
throttle_retry.note: local policy pinned by this seed
